label: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3

extraction.one_line_summary: TARAMAY Ryan attacked from 8-rider breakaway on Picon Blanco's 18% ramps to win and take red jersey
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC/Jumbo-Visma deliberately allowed 8-rider break containing GC threats to gain massive time, refusing chase duty despite virtual leader risk.
TARAMAY at 34 years old proved superior to DOMBROWSKI on steepest 18% ramps, suggesting age matters less than power profile on extreme gradients.
Break composition with multiple GC-capable riders forced peloton paralysis; no single team willing to burn matches controlling virtual race lead.

rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: Olympic champion struggling on Picon Blanco raises serious questions about grand tour form and GC viability
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Strong performance while teammate Carapaz suffered suggests Ineos has wrong GC leader designated
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: Stage 2 crash consequences materializing with time loss on first mountain test, deficit compounding dangerously early
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Bauke: neutral: Mentioned in break but struggled on steepest ramps, confirming power limitations on 18%+ gradients
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Taking young rider jersey while finishing with GC group signals form building despite subdued racing
